title: Smoky Fluffy Kenyan Shrimp Agemono
description: A Kenyan-inspired shrimp dish with a smoky flavor from the shrimp paste and a fluffy texture from the agemono-fry.

ingredients.name: Baking Soda
ingredients.notes: To help the shrimp paste adhere.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: To bring out the flavor of the shrimp.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: To add depth to the dish.

instructions: Dust the large shrimp with all-purpose flour, shaking off any excess.
Mix the shrimp paste, baking soda, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
Coat each shrimp with the shrimp paste mixture, pressing it onto the shrimp to adhere.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 35010F.
Carefully add the coated shrimp to the hot oil and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried shrimp from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.
